etymology.description: semantic 肉 (肉) (ròu, "body") + phonetic 尃 (尃) (fū) -> arm
etymology.interpretation: The character 膊 combines 肉(肉) (ròu, "body"), indicating a part of the anatomy, with 尃(尃) (fū) as the phonetic component. The semantic component emphasizes its meaning related to the body, specifically referring to the 'arm' as a body part.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 膊 directly retains the traditional components without further simplification.

examples.chinese: 他的膊非常强壮(他的膊非常強壯)。
她举起了双手，露出了膊(她舉起了雙手，露出了膊)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jǔ qǐ le shuāng shǒu, lùchūle bó.
examples.english: She raised her hands, revealing her arms.

examples.chinese: 那个舞蹈动作需要灵活的膊(那個舞蹈動作需要靈活的膊)。
examples.pinyin: Nàge wǔdǎo dòngzuò xūyào línghuó de bó.
examples.english: That dance move requires flexible arms.
